channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canada explores Saab aircraft purchase amid U.S. defence concerns
item.description: Canada has recently spent over $65 billion on defence and reached NATO’s 2 per cent of GDP spending target, yet the United States has expressed dissatisfaction with Canada’s defence commitments. On Ma...

item.title: Makonda appoints Haji Manara as spokesperson for Tanzania national football teams
item.description: The Minister for Information, Culture, Arts and Sports, Paul Makonda, has officially appointed Haji Manara as the new spokesperson for all Tanzania national football teams operating under the National...

item.title: Jannik Sinner loses to Juan Manuel Cerundolo in second round at French Open
item.description: Jannik Sinner, world No 1 and favourite for this year’s French Open, suffered a dramatic second-round defeat to Argentina's Juan Manuel Cerundolo, despite leading 6-3, 6-2, 5-1. Sinner described feeli...

item.title: Karseell Collagen and Argan Oil Hair Mask Available at Discount for Frizz Control
item.description: A popular collagen and argan oil hair mask from the brand Karseell is currently on sale at Amazon for $19.01, representing a 24% discount from its regular price of $24.86. The product has received str...

item.title: SARB raises repo rate to 7% amid rising inflation risks driven by oil and food prices
item.description: The South African Reserve Bank (SARB) has increased its main policy interest rate by 25 basis points to 7%, citing growing inflation risks linked to rising global oil prices and persistent pressure on...

item.title: PCCB stops illegal land transfer worth over Sh3 billion in Arusha
item.description: The Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB) in Arusha has successfully stopped an illegal land transaction involving three plots valued at over Sh3 billion. The plots, located in Levolosi...

item.title: Fitch revises sovereign debt rules to clarify treatment of temporary payment pauses
item.description: This article explains how Fitch Ratings, one of the major global credit rating agencies, has updated its framework for assessing sovereign debt situations, especially when countries temporarily pause ...

item.title: Piki Lopez addresses Lopez family dispute during First Gen annual stockholders’ meeting
item.description: For the first time since the Lopez cousins' public feud began in March, Federico “Piki” Lopez appeared at First Gen Corporation’s virtual annual stockholders’ meeting on May 28, 2026. During the sessi...

item.title: Update on South Africa’s ‘KFC couple’ seven years later
item.description: Seven years after becoming a viral sensation in South Africa, Hector and Nonhlanhla Mkansi, widely known as the ‘KFC couple’, are still together and continuing their life as a family of five. The coup...

item.title: NDP Leader Avi Lewis distinguishes between Alberta and Quebec separatist movements
item.description: OTTAWA — NDP Leader Avi Lewis clarified that the Sherbrooke declaration, a foundational policy recognizing Quebec’s right to self-determination, does not extend to Alberta. Lewis dismissed Alberta's s...

item.title: Homicide investigation launched into Belconnen unit fire death of 46-year-old woman
item.description: The death of 46-year-old Kylee McHugh, who was found after a unit fire in Belconnen, Canberra, is now being investigated as a homicide. Emergency services were called to the Wallcliffe Flats on Osborn...

item.title: Remo Stars plan rebuilding after historic relegation from NPFL
item.description: Remo Stars F.C., once Nigeria Premier Football League champions, now face life in the second division after a dramatic relegation confirmed by a late 1-1 draw with Bendel Insurance in Benin City. Vict...

item.title: Dr Ranj Singh reflects on coming out as gay, the end of his marriage and finding support in the LGBT community
item.description: Dr Ranj Singh, the well-known TV doctor, has opened up about the personal challenges he faced after coming out as gay at the age of 30, an event that coincided with the breakdown of his seven-year mar...

item.title: Dating apps use gas giveaways to combat dating affordability crisis
item.description: The WIRED article examines how economic pressures in 2026 are shaping dating culture, particularly for Gen Z and lower-income singles. With gas prices surging to around $4.56 per gallon and broader en...

item.title: Tanzania Volleyball Federation to Start Monthly Beach Volleyball Tournaments at Coco Beach
item.description: The Tanzania Volleyball Federation (TVF) has announced new plans to organise monthly beach volleyball tournaments at Coco Beach in Dar es Salaam. This move comes after the country successfully hosted ...

item.title: NF1 research breakthrough sparks hope for simple, affordable therapies
item.description: Zoe Petropoulos, a 25-year-old teacher’s aide from Australia, has spent years fundraising for neurofibromatosis (NF) research through the Flicker of Hope Foundation. Her family’s efforts, totaling ove...

item.title: Three killed in separate motorcycle crashes in Pangasinan
item.description: Three people died and one other was injured in a series of separate road accidents across Pangasinan over a span of two days, highlighting ongoing concerns over motorcycle-related crashes in the provi...

item.title: Study Confirms Turtles Are Closely Related to Birds and Crocodiles
item.description: Scientists don finally figure out where turtles belong for the tree of life after many years of confusion. For long, genetic studies show say turtles dey related to archosaurs, the group wey include m...

item.title: How FIFA Prepares and Maintains Perfect World Cup Turf
item.description: As the FIFA World Cup approaches, the quality of the playing field is receiving unprecedented attention. Millions of dollars and years of research have gone into developing the ideal grass for the wor...

item.title: Zanzibar increases funding and reforms to strengthen gender-based violence and child protection programmes
item.description: Zanzibar’s Ministry of Community Development, Gender, Elderly and Children has announced a major expansion of its efforts to combat gender-based violence (GBV) and improve child protection services ac...
